fields.POSITIONING_BEST: Teams adding AI into search, observability, or security workflows.
fields.POSITIONING_NOT_FIT: Teams wanting a standalone machine learning platform.
fields.POSITIONING_START: Teams asking how to use AI in real production systems.
fields.POSITIONING_LENS: Evaluate with real AI use cases, platform fit, and operational simplicity.

fields.POSITIONING_BEST: Applications needing fast, relevant search across large data sets.
fields.POSITIONING_NOT_FIT: Small apps where basic database search already works.
fields.POSITIONING_START: Teams complaining search is slow or results are poor.
fields.POSITIONING_LENS: Evaluate with search scale, relevance needs, and user experience impact.

fields.POSITIONING_BEST: Complex systems where outages are hard to diagnose.
fields.POSITIONING_NOT_FIT: Simple environments with few services and rare incidents.
fields.POSITIONING_START: Teams saying incidents take too long to resolve.
fields.POSITIONING_LENS: Evaluate with incident pain, system complexity, and visibility gaps.

fields.POSITIONING_BEST: Security teams replacing or modernizing a legacy SIEM.
fields.POSITIONING_NOT_FIT: Organizations with minimal monitoring or very small SOC teams.
fields.POSITIONING_START: Teams overwhelmed by alerts or high SIEM cost.
fields.POSITIONING_LENS: Evaluate with alert volume, investigation speed, and SIEM cost pressure.

fields.POSITIONING_BEST: Organizations trying to consolidate search, observability, and security tools.
fields.POSITIONING_NOT_FIT: Teams satisfied managing many separate data platforms.
fields.POSITIONING_START: Leaders asking how to simplify operational data systems.
fields.POSITIONING_LENS: Evaluate with consolidation goals, data scale, and cross team usage.
